title: Rootless 'podman exec' fails with containers that are both privileged and have "host" networking
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

This is a continuation of #1313 

I have a script like this to create a container called `fedora-toolbox-rishi:28`:
```
#!/bin/sh

toolbox_container="fedora-toolbox-$USER:28"

base_toolbox_image="registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:28"
toolbox_image="fedora-toolbox-$USER:28"
working_container_name="fedora-toolbox-$USER-working-container"

buildah images --noheading | grep --quiet $toolbox_image
if [[ $? -eq 1 ]]; then
  buildah from --name $working_container_name $base_toolbox_image
  buildah containers --noheading | grep --quiet $working_container_name
  [[ $? -eq 1 ]] && echo "Failed to create working container" && exit 1

  buildah run $working_container_name -- useradd \
      --no-create-home \
      --uid $UID \
      --groups wheel \
      $USER
  buildah config --volume $HOME $working_container_name
  buildah config --volume /run/user/$UID $working_container_name

  buildah config --user $USER $working_container_name
  buildah config --workingdir $HOME $working_container_name

  buildah commit --rm $working_container_name $toolbox_image
fi

podman create \
    --hostname toolbox \
    --interactive \
    --name $toolbox_container \
    --network host \
    --privileged \
    --security-opt label=disable \
    --tty \
    --volume $HOME:$HOME \
    --volume /run/user/$UID:/run/user/$UID \
    $toolbox_image
```

This is what happens when I used it to create the container, started it, and tried to `exec` a shell inside it:
```
$ ./toolbox-create 
6a7e6ae6ac35f4e97557c2f31004ad4c99edd96d4b9ddfdb807cdc65acb7ede8
$ podman start fedora-toolbox-rishi:28 
fedora-toolbox-rishi:28
$ podman exec --interactive --tty fedora-toolbox-rishi:28 /bin/bash -l
exec failed: container_linux.go:336: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:86: executing setns process caused \"exit status 21\""
exit status 1
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.8.3
Go Version:    go1.10.3
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.8.3-4.1.git9d09a4d.fc28.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: 85417f7b2b50fe6202ef7cf222751d0b522a30aa-dirty'
  MemFree: 11936907264
  MemTotal: 16696360960
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-46.dev.gitb4e2ecb.fc28.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.0'
  SwapFree: 4208979968
  SwapTotal: 4208979968
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: bollard
  kernel: 4.17.14-202.fc28.x86_64
  os: linux
  uptime: 7m 45.4s
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions: []
  GraphRoot: /var/home/rishi/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 3
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000/run
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

This is a physical laptop running Fedora 28 Silverblue 28.20180823.0.

comments.body:
I'm not able to reproduce this with a simpler test case:

```
$ sudo podman run -dt --net=host --privileged alpine top
d71c3590fb5ef0255086d0a4e724a509349b57c3bd6533e9c8ff6a092dbee2dd

$ sudo podman exec -it -l ls
bin    dev    etc    home   lib    media  mnt    proc   root   run    sbin   srv    sys    tmp    usr    var
$
```

Can you try just the above and report back?

If I drop the `sudo`, then I get the same failure:
```
$ podman run -dt --net=host --privileged alpine top
Trying to pull docker.io/alpine:latest...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:8e3ba11ec2a2b39ab372c60c16b421536e50e5ce64a0bc81765c2e38381bcff6
 2.10 MB / 2.10 MB [========================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:11cd0b38bc3ceb958ffb2f9bd70be3fb317ce7d255c8a4c3f4af30e298aa1aab
 1.48 KB / 1.48 KB [========================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
9212ab41a1e89455fe1199ed5ee5baf119c43aad4f9610d5d0b678bf669efb49
$ podman exec -it -l ls
exec failed: container_linux.go:336: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:86: executing setns process caused \"exit status 21\""
exit status 1
```
I am afraid this is going to be ugly, we need to do the "exec" from the same user namespace that originally created the container.  Taking a look right now
Yes, PR #1331 fixed the problem for me.
